summary: react-simple-utils-kit exfiltrates Coze API credentials and workspace data on install
details: Package advertises itself as a React date-formatting utility but ships a postinstall.js that runs automatically on npm install. The script reads Coze-related environment variables (COZE_WORKLOAD_API_TOKEN, COZE_PROJECT_SPACE_ID, COZE_PROJECT_ID, COZE_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_CLIENT_ID/SECRET), uses the token to enumerate workspaces, bots, workflows, knowledge bases, conversations, API keys, and plugins from api.coze.cn, and POSTs the responses — including freshly-minted access tokens and the full API-key inventory — to a hardcoded attacker-controlled capture endpoint at http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com over cleartext HTTP. The advertised date-utility functionality in index.js is unrelated to the postinstall behavior and serves as cover. Installer harm: any developer or build system with Coze credentials in environment variables loses those credentials and the associated workspace contents to the attacker on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
